page: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache per core, with support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and AES cryptography.

The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 19 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is strong across both small and large block read/write operations, ranking in the top 25%. The system also exhibits top-tier performance in software compilation and image processing, alongside strong results in database operations, Redis SET operations, and medium-model LLM inference prompt processing.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and DDR5 memory generation, the r7a.12xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads requiring high resource density without the overhead of local NVMe storage. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale language model inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor with 48 dedicated physical cores operating at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth, with excellent results in software compilation, image processing, and database operations. This hardware profile makes the r7a.12xlarge highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory caches, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 19 Gbps network baseline, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized, high-frequency AMD EPYC instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and DDR5 memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores operating at 3.7 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and software compilation performance
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis, and memory-bound workloads
